resources.string: Unable to open the selected attachment.
This attachment type is not supported.
Please respond to the provided attachments using the current context.
The selected model does not support one or more attached media types.
The shared media was received, but the selected model does not support that modality yet.
The shared request did not contain usable text or media.
Some staged media were removed because the selected model no longer supports them.
No external source details were needed for this request.
No concrete action has been selected yet.
No approval step is currently active.
No major context contributors are active right now.
No managed knowledge support is active for this request.
No runtime contributor outcome is available for this request yet.
No extension contribution is active for this request.
No additional limiting condition is active right now.
No recent runtime activity has been captured yet.
No active memory is being pulled into this request.
No managed knowledge asset has been ingested yet.
No recent governance change is active.
Approvals stay inline with the active chat flow.
Review the next approval from the inline approval surface.
No extension contribution is currently visible.
Extension editing is not part of this control-center slice yet.
No system-source contribution is active right now.
Additional device permissions are needed for fuller context.
Ingest durable local sources, keep retrieval explainable, and manage freshness separately from memory.
No managed knowledge source has been added yet.
No readable local source could be indexed.
%1$s is not yet a supported text knowledge source.
Matched this request at retrieval time.
Select at least one local source to ingest.
That knowledge asset is no longer available.
Could not update retrieval inclusion for %1$s.
No managed knowledge source is available yet.
Knowledge retrieval was skipped because this request did not include searchable text.
No managed knowledge strongly matched this request.
One or more knowledge sources are missing or no longer readable.
Some knowledge sources were only partially ingested.
One or more knowledge sources are currently excluded from retrieval.
One or more knowledge sources may be stale.
Not used in a request yet.
No active memory matched this request yet.
%1$d private item(s) remain redacted from this view.
Start the first local exchange when the model is ready.
Pick a starter below or type your own request to begin the session.
Choose an existing local model file and copy it into this app.
This clears the visible conversation and starts a fresh local session.
Turn the day so far into a short recap with open loops.
Use the current context to suggest the clearest next actions.
Explain what is loaded right now and why it matters.
Routing request through the runtime session pipeline.
Fresh local session ready. No prior turns yet.
Active runtime session with %1$d visible turns.
Select a local model to start the workspace.
Import a .litertlm model to enable real local generation.
Imported file is missing. Re-import the model to use it.
Unable to open the selected file.
Accepted request into the local runtime.
%1$d transcript turns available for runtime planning.
No compatible provider is registered for `%1$s`.
Runtime session was cancelled before completion.
The local execution gate denied this request.
Execution requires explicit approval before continuing.
The local execution gate approved this request.
Policy resolved this request as safe to auto-execute.
Policy paused this request until you respond to the approval prompt.
Policy denied this request before execution started.
Approval rejected. Execution did not continue.
Approval was dismissed. Execution did not continue.
Approval timed out. Execution did not continue.
Failed to initialize the local model runtime.
Selected model is not runtime-ready. Import a .litertlm model first.
Unsupported model format for chat. Please import a .litertlm model.
Unable to start %1$s on this device. Try switching models or retrying.
without changing the top-level session contract.
Using persona defaults with no active memory.
Active context assembled from in-scope persona and memory.
Active context assembled with private evidence redacted from the workspace summary.
Active context assembled from the highest-signal in-scope memory items.
Attach scoped memory and persona context at request time.
Local-first contributor with no external caller trust requirement.
Only app-scoped, contact-scoped, and device-scoped memory may attach.
Private memory stays local and only contributes request-time summaries.
This contributor stays request-time only and does not imply corpus sync or export.
Retrieve durable local knowledge with visible citations and limits.
Local managed corpus with no external caller trust requirement.
Only explicitly ingested knowledge assets may contribute at request time.
Request-time support stays source-linked and can downgrade to summary-only citations.
Knowledge support remains explainable and separate from memory or automation state.
Apply trust, scope, and approval checks before execution.
Requires runtime trust and policy evaluation.
Evaluates the effective tool scope before execution continues.
This gate can block, degrade, or pause execution before a tool runs.
Disabled from the runtime contributor surface.
No request-time context was attached for this contributor.
A required permission or dependency is still unavailable.
No managed contributor explanation is available yet.
Requires privileged local context and device access.
Prefers direct progress when the action is low risk.
The user prefers concise planning updates and short action summaries.
The current focus is implementing the local runtime and scoped memory system.
Current focus is runtime and memory implementation.
Alice asked for short text updates instead of voice notes this week.
Private records must remain local and cannot be exported.
This record may leave the device only as a summary.
This record may export full content when explicitly requested.
Compatible with the current metadata contract.
%1$d extension hook(s) evaluated for current records.
Portability bundle is ready to share.
Unable to open Android share for this bundle.
Classifier marked %1$s as medium risk and recommended review.
Classifier marked %1$s as high risk and recommended confirmation.
%1$s can continue automatically under the current policy.
Policy could not fully normalize this request, so a preview is required first.
Policy requires a preview before continuing because the request is medium risk.
Policy requires explicit confirmation because the request is high risk.
%1$s is about to continue. Original request: %2$s
Received content from %1$s, but the selected model is not ready yet.
No structured payload for this request.
AppFunctions provider is unavailable for this request.
Real AppFunctions provider is available on this device.
This device does not support real AppFunctions yet. Falling back.
Mobile Claw AppFunctions service is not registered.
No generated AppFunctions metadata matched this capability.
The mapped AppFunction exists but is currently disabled.
Real AppFunctions discovery failed, so fallback routing remains active.
Intent fallback is available in degraded mode.
Structured normalization does not apply to this capability.
Normalized the request into message fields before execution.
Normalized the request into calendar fields before execution.
Normalized the request into share fields before execution.
Recipient could not be confidently identified.
Content could not be confidently identified.
Scheduling time is incomplete or missing.
Event title is incomplete or missing.
Share destination is missing or ambiguous.
Generate a local reply or draft without changing device state.
Read schedule context from the device calendar.
Prepare or dispatch a calendar event write.
Prepare or dispatch a new alarm.
Open the alarm surface without mutating alarm state.
Prepare or dispatch an alarm dismissal request.
Prepare or dispatch a message send action.
Prepare or dispatch content to another app through sharing.
A runtime action that has not been standardized yet.
This tool is relevant, but no usable binding is currently available.
No runtime-ready local model is available for AppFunctions reply generation.
AppFunctions reply generation completed without text output.
The requested memory record could not be found for portability export.
%1$s is allowed but should remain reviewable.
%1$s may request %2$s, but it should stay reviewable.
%1$d upcoming calendar item(s) contributed to context.
No system source contributed to this request.
Caller is trusted for this request.
Caller is trusted for restricted capability routing.
Caller failed verification for restricted capability routing.
Caller package %1$s is not installed on this device.
Caller identity is unverified, so only low-risk flows may continue.
No capability registration matched this request.
No eligible Android provider is available for %1$s.
Capability route metadata is missing for execution.
Capability %1$s is not supported by Android intent execution.
No Android app is available to handle this action.
Failed to launch the selected Android app.
Source package %1$s has not been verified yet.
Source package was not available for verification.
This external handoff type is not supported yet: %1$s.
The incoming handoff did not include any text content.
The incoming handoff could not be normalized safely.
Contains %1$d unknown field(s); safe compatibility mode applied.
Request version %1$s does not match runtime version %2$s.
Callable surface %1$s is not available on this runtime.
No shared content grants were provided.
%1$s Access is expected to expire with the current session.
You are Mobile Claw, a local-first personal Android agent runtime.
Answer the latest request using the persona and memory above when relevant.
Create a reusable local flow, then run and resume it here.
Enable this workflow before running it.
Add at least one workflow step before activation.
Enable at least one entry trigger before activation.
Knowledge-aware steps need at least one included knowledge asset.
Approval granted. The workflow can continue.
Approval rejected. The workflow stopped without applying the next step.
Approval timed out. Resume when you are ready.
Approval dismissed. Resume when you want to continue.
User approval is required before continuing.
Add or re-enable knowledge support, then resume.
Review the failure and resume or restart if needed.
The run is paused locally and can be resumed later.
Approve, reject, or leave this run paused until later.
Resume this run to request approval again and continue.
Start a new run when you want to try again.
Start another run when you need the same workflow again.
Review the blocked step, then resume or create a fresh run.
The app was interrupted. Resume from the latest checkpoint.
Restore workflow dependencies, then resume from this guard step.
A guard step blocked the workflow until dependencies recover.
Approval was requested for the next step.
Approval was granted and the workflow resumed.
Approval was rejected and the workflow stopped.
Approval was dismissed and the workflow became resumable.
Prepared a reusable knowledge briefing outcome.
Prepared a reusable follow-up action outcome.
The workflow was interrupted and marked resumable.
Paused the workflow without losing checkpoint state.
Resumed the workflow from its latest checkpoint.
This run was cancelled before the remaining steps executed.
%1$s finished all of its configured steps locally.
The workflow could not complete the current step.
The knowledge-aware guard could not continue because no included knowledge asset is available.
The selected workflow template is no longer available.
This workflow definition is no longer available.
This workflow run is no longer available.
Resolve the existing active run before starting another one.
This workflow run cannot be resumed right now.
Load context, guard for knowledge availability, require approval, then produce a reusable briefing outcome.
Collect recent context, pause for approval, then prepare a reusable follow-up outcome.
Collect current memory and any allowed knowledge support before continuing.
Make sure knowledge-aware prerequisites are still available.
Pause and ask for confirmation before promoting the prepared outcome.
Pause and ask for confirmation before preparing the next follow-up action.
Produce a concise reusable briefing outcome for the next task pass.
Produce a concise reusable follow-up outcome for the next action pass.
Continue into approval if dependencies remain healthy.
Continue into the local action step after approval.
Persist the run outcome and close the workflow.
